reviews.review_description:
i like the game.  its a good workout that brings your heart rate up fast.  it's relatively different from all the other exercise games and i like that it adds variety.  that said there are some things i would like to see improved. 
1 infinite runners are boring.  id prefer levels so i could feel a sense of accomplishment.  infinite should just be one mode.  even if you use the random generator just at different speeds, and just save them into 100 levels for people to go through, i think it would add interest. it would also let us restart at harder levels.
2 let us know when we are about to die.  is it 3 hits?  but you sometimes replenish?  id like to know the details.  show us hit points and perhaps replenish by collecting metal gears.  that would help gamify the workout more.
3 some inconsistencies.  i feel like i blocked or dodged but i get hit, don't always know why.  the tutorial on block isn't clear, and i saw the developer say "boxing stance" but I'm not a boxer.  an example fix is showing shadows like in oh shape so you know if you might hit a wall.
4 id prefer to actually jump than wave my hands to jump.  id suggest that be an option.  this isn't wii balance board where you can't actually jump - we can jump here!
over all great but could be improved with a little more work.  good luck!

developer_response.body:
Hi PJ, glad you enjoyed the game!

1) Factory Run is at its core an infinite runner, creating levels aren't feasible. You can use modifiers to start at a harder level.
2) The railings on either side are your fail indicators, going from green, orange and red. You will slowly move forward after a hit. The faster the conveyor belt, the further you're thrown back.
3) Make sure to move fully out the way of obstacles. You can crash through wooden crates, however metal containers will knock you back.
4) Unfortunately this is not possible.

Thank you for your feedback!

reviews.review_description:
Like your game and think it has a lot of potential! 
Is there a chance you can add a no fail mode?  I start to get my heart rate up but then inevitably lose at some point.  By the time I play again my heart rate has already come down.
Thanks,

developer_response.body:
Hi Jake,
Thanks for the suggestion! Yes that's definitely something we'll add in future updates.

reviews.review_title: A fun fitness game at a decent price
reviews.review_description: A rigorous workout as you jump, duck, dodge & punch your way along a series of obstacle-filled conveyor belts within a factory setting. Good responses & accurate readings of your movements combine to produce a sweat-induced workout that will shed the pounds.

My preteens tried to play the game before I did, but they had a problem with the controls. I was able to figure out how to play and I definitely worked up a sweat. 
No doubt worth the price.

I wanted to like this game. I couldn't get passed the tutorial. The part where it says guard your face to break the boxes. Nothing I did seemed right. It also seemed like it was looking for a specific angle. 

Not being able to pass the tutorial was discouraging. The game is fairly simple. It's a smaller size game. The background stays the same for the most part where a conveyor belt brings boxes towards you. The power ups to break boxes have to be purchased with in game money not real money. 

I can see it get boring and repetitive pretty fast. Music was upbeat and nice to listen to with the beat but it's the same

developer_response.body:
Hi Kloudy,
Guard your face in a boxing stance to crash through wooden crates. Make sure not to hit metal containers!

reviews.review_description: I Love this game makes you sweat and it makes you want to go faster and faster I do wish there was a way if you miss up to 3 times before you start over when your moving and mess up it kinda makes you wish you could start again on same level  As long as you don’t leave game. I want to tell the creator awesome job ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
A fun game that makes you sweat. The tutorial is a must. Lots of entertainment while you workout!

A must-have for anyone who wants to break a sweat, get fit and have fun whilst doing it! This game turns fitness into an adventure with its energetic mix of jumping, ducking, weaving, and punching your way whilst stood on a conveyor belt. Who knew burning calories could be this much fun?  I was puffing and panting within five minutes and I have discovered getting fit with no rhythm is like dancing to elevator music—awkward, but at least you're moving!  A leader board would be good; if it’s there I missed it.  Also you really do need to do the tutorial!!

reviews.review_title: Fitness fun…  on a conveyor belt
reviews.review_description:
This is fun…. Dodging left to right, ducking and jumping… punching the matching coloured cogs as you go…. Faster… Faster!!!

It’s quite simple really as that’s all there is…. Keep going to see how far you can make it.

Nice to follow tutorial to help you get going, then it’s away we go… nice and slowly at first, but things soon start to pick up.

Can really work up a sweat on this and have fun doing fitness.

I did at first have problems with the cover face to smash through the crates.. I tried all different combinations and none worked… but I then realised you have to lean towards the wooden crates and cover face to smash them ones, as the other crates are steel and can’t be smashed.

A reviewer before me, also had a problem with this… hope he sees my solution.

One of those hidden gems you come across to discover this is actually a lot of fun.
After a short tutorial, you dive into a pulsating workout, place your feet and away you go, 
Standing on the conveyor belt, you punch and weave your way passed boxes, signs and fences, 
You do work up a good sweat, squats and punches are the order of the day.
The game is very well priced and mechanics really are on point.
Well done to the dev.

I keep running but don't seem to be going anywhere... funny how that works.
Anyway it's a neat game, more challenging than it looks.
Fun Game. It's your typical infinite running game but with a VR twist. Though it is surprising how tiring it can get, but at least the music is groovy.

reviews.review_title: it’s got a lot of potential..
reviews.review_description: I like the game, but I can’t get very far because I can’t seem to block my way through the crates. I put my hands up in front of my face and body and every possible combination, but it never seems to work :(

developer_response.body:
Hi Andy,
To block put your hands in front of your face, like in a boxing stance. Make sure to lean into the wooden crates - you can't destroy the metal containers!

reviews.review_description:
There’s definitely potential with this as a workout game. My main issue is that the mechanics are inconsistent and break the game. Half of the time the jump motion does nothing. I’ve tried doing it faster, slower, higher, lower- nothing. It’s impossible to put a good run together when one of the core mechanics seems to be broken. 

Not eligible for a refund so I will return after some updates to see if it’s been fixed

developer_response.body:
Hi muddyisland,

In order to jump you need to swing your arms in an upward motion. If you're having difficulty you can increase the hand sensitivity in the Shop.
